# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.

Prevent hardcoded secrets.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

# config/initializers/sendgrid_mailer.rb
require 'rails'
 
class SendgridMailer < ActionMailer::Base   # Inherits from Rails Base Mailer class for ease of use and reusability in other classes or actions within the same application (ActionPack) module, etc...). This is a base mailers to be used as an example. You can extend it with your own specific needs
  include SendGrid::Mailer
  
  # Define default from address for all emails sent by this Mailer class and set SMTP settings here: https://sendgrid.com/docs/for-developers/articles/how-to-set-up-email-with-rails_mailer/. You can use environment variables or secrets manager to store sensitive data like API keys, etc...
  default from: 'from@example.com' # This is the email address that will be shown in "From" field of emails sent by this mailer class and also as a sender for any other mails generated through it (unless overridden). You can use environment variables or secrets manager to store sensitive data like API keys, etc...
  
  def welcome_email(user) # Define method with user object parameter. This is the email recipient in this case: https://sendgrid.com/docs/for-developers/articles/how-to-set-up-recipients/. You can use environment variables or secrets manager to store sensitive data like API keys, etc...
    mail(to: user.email,  # Set recipient email address here (user object parameter). This is the one that will receive this kind of emails sent by your SendgridMailer class instance and also as a sender for any other mails generated through it or its subclasses in Rails applications if you have set up default from settings correctly.
         subject: 'Welcome to My App!') # Set email's title here (subject parameter). This is the one that will be displayed on your emails clients and also as a sender for any other mails generated through it or its subclasses in Rails applications if you have set up default from settings correctly.
  end  
end
